<<<KOT:raininess>>>>
{"kind":"molecule","gloss":"the condition when much {urn:molecule-v0:water|water} [m] comes down from the {urn:molecule-v0:sky|sky} [m] at some times; this happens in one way; people feel this when they see it and feel something about it.","groundingNote":"the condition when much {urn:molecule-v0:water|water} [m] comes down from the {urn:molecule-v0:sky|sky} [m] at some times; this happens in one way; people feel this when they see it and feel something about it.","groundingRefs":["urn:molecule-v0:water","urn:molecule-v0:sky"]}

<<<KOT:reductionism>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"reductionism is a philosophical position about explanation and analysis — holding that complex systems can be fully explained by their simpler parts. It is not grounded in concrete percepts, actions, or things; it requires meta-cognitive concepts like 'explanation', 'parts', 'system', and 'reduction' that are not in the prime lexicon. The everyday sense (if any) would be a theoretical stance, not a phenomenon that can be anchored to molecular or prime-based scaffolding."}
<<<END:reductionism>>>>

<<<KOT:reticulocyte>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"a reticulocyte is a technical hematological kind — an immature red blood cell characterized by a reticular network of ribosomal RNA visible only under specialized microscopy. Its identity conditions (developmental stage in erythropoiesis, RNA content, staining properties, bone-marrow origin) require medical and molecular biology concepts not expressible in the prime lexicon or available catalog. This belongs to the medical/cellular sector, not the grounding tier."}
<<<END:reticulocyte>>>>

<<<KOT:romanticistic>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"romanticistic is an adjective referring to a relationship to romanticism — itself a historical artistic and philosophical movement. It is a culture-bound scholarly term describing adherence to or association with a specific intellectual tradition, not a property or state grounded in percepts, actions, or bodily experience. It cannot be anchored to the prime lexicon or everyday sensory phenomena."}
<<<END:romanticistic>>>>
